checkpoints.title: Structured logging library present
checkpoints.description: Service should use a structured logging library to produce machine-parseable log output (JSON or key-value). Unstructured logs are difficult to query and alert on.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a structured logging library: Python: structlog or loguru. Node.js: pino or winston. Go: zap or zerolog. Java: logback with JSON encoder. .NET: Serilog.

checkpoints.title: Log level configurable via environment
checkpoints.description: Log verbosity must be configurable at runtime via an environment variable so it can be adjusted without a code change.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Read log level from an environment variable: Python: logging.getLevelName(os.environ.get('LOG_LEVEL', 'INFO')). Node.js: process.env.LOG_LEVEL. Go: os.Getenv('LOG_LEVEL').

checkpoints.title: Distributed tracing SDK present
checkpoints.description: Service should emit distributed traces so requests can be followed across service boundaries.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add OpenTelemetry (vendor-neutral, recommended): Python: opentelemetry-sdk. Node.js: @opentelemetry/sdk-node. Go: go.opentelemetry.io/otel. Java: opentelemetry-java.

checkpoints.title: Health or readiness endpoint
checkpoints.description: Web services must expose a health endpoint used by load balancers and orchestration platforms for liveness and readiness probes.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a /health endpoint returning HTTP 200 when the service is healthy. Include a /ready endpoint if your service has startup dependencies.

checkpoints.title: Metrics instrumentation present
checkpoints.description: Service should emit metrics (request count, latency, error rate) so dashboards and alerts can be built.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add metrics instrumentation. OpenTelemetry Metrics is vendor-neutral. Prometheus client libraries are widely supported. At minimum, track request count, latency (p50/p99), and error rate.

checkpoints.title: Monitoring dashboard configured
checkpoints.description: Service must have a production monitoring dashboard showing the four golden signals: request rate, error rate, latency (p99), and saturation.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a monitoring dashboard (Grafana, Datadog, CloudWatch, Azure Monitor) and attest in .readiness/external-evidence.json with attestation_key 'monitoring_dashboard' and a link to the dashboard.

checkpoints.title: Alerting configured
checkpoints.description: Service must have at least one alert defined for critical failure conditions (error rate spike, latency degradation, or service down).
checkpoints.fix_hint: Define alerts for your service (PagerDuty, OpsGenie, Grafana Alerting, CloudWatch Alarms) and attest in .readiness/external-evidence.json with attestation_key 'alerting_configured' and a link to the alert configuration.

checkpoints.title: On-call / incident registration
checkpoints.description: Service must be registered in the incident management system with an on-call rotation so there is a clear owner when things go wrong.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Register your service in your incident management platform (PagerDuty, OpsGenie, ICM, VictorOps) with an on-call schedule and escalation policy. Attest in .readiness/external-evidence.json with attestation_key 'incident_registration'.
